infobox.value: (Polish: [[kaˈba.tɘ̟]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Polish "Help:IPA/Polish")) is a [neighbourhood](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw"), and a [City Information System](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") area, in [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw"), Poland, within the [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") district. It is a predominantly high-rise residential area with housing eststes of apartment buildings, with a smaller presence of single-family housing, including [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw "Moczydło, Warsaw") in the northwest, and [Stare Kabaty](/wiki/Stare_Kabaty "Stare Kabaty") in the east. In 2024, it had around 30,000 residents. The neighbourhood includes the [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty_metro_station "Kabaty metro station") station of the [M1](/wiki/M1_(Warsaw) "M1 (Warsaw)") line of [Warsaw Metro](/wiki/Warsaw_Metro "Warsaw Metro") rapid transit underground system.

**Kabaty** (Polish: [[kaˈba.tɘ̟]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Polish "Help:IPA/Polish")) is a [neighbourhood](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw"), and a [City Information System](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") area, in [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw"), Poland, within the [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") district. It is a predominantly high-rise residential area with housing eststes of apartment buildings, with a smaller presence of single-family housing, including [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw "Moczydło, Warsaw") in the northwest, and [Stare Kabaty](/wiki/Stare_Kabaty "Stare Kabaty") in the east. In 2024, it had around 30,000 residents. The neighbourhood includes the [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty_metro_station "Kabaty metro station") station of the [M1](/wiki/M1_(Warsaw) "M1 (Warsaw)") line of [Warsaw Metro](/wiki/Warsaw_Metro "Warsaw Metro") rapid transit underground system.

The oldest known records of Kabady come from 1386, when it was a small farming community. By 1527, the village of Moczydło was also present to the west. The area was incorporated into Warsaw in 1951. In the 1980s, and continuing to the 2000s, numerous housing estates were developed within the area. In 1995, the Kabaty station opened within the neighbourhood.

## Toponomy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kabaty&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Toponomy")]

The name *Kabaty* comes from the [Polish surname](/wiki/Polish_surnames "Polish surnames") *Kabat*, which itself comes from [Polish](/wiki/Polish_language "Polish language") word *kabat*, an archaic term for a short coat. The neighbourhood is also a namesake of the nearby [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods"), the largest nature reserve within Warsaw.[[1]](#cite_note-handke-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kabaty&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: History")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/17/Henryk_Podd%C4%99bski_-_Chata_w_Kabatach_%28131-1926%29.jpg/250px-Henryk_Podd%C4%99bski_-_Chata_w_Kabatach_%28131-1926%29.jpg)](/wiki/File:Henryk_Podd%C4%99bski_-_Chata_w_Kabatach_(131-1926).jpg)

The village of Kabaty in 1926.

The oldest known records of Kabady come from 1386, when it received [Kulm law](/wiki/Kulm_law "Kulm law") privileges from duke [Janusz I the Old](/wiki/Janusz_I_the_Old "Janusz I the Old"), ruler of the [Duchy of Warsaw](/wiki/Duchy_of_Warsaw_(Middle_Ages) "Duchy of Warsaw (Middle Ages)"). It was a small farming community, located at the edge of the Warsaw Escarpment, and on a road connecting [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw") and [Czersk](/wiki/Czersk,_Masovian_Voivodeship "Czersk, Masovian Voivodeship"). The village was owned by the [Ciołek family](/wiki/Cio%C5%82ek_coat_of_arms "Ciołek coat of arms") until 17th century, when it was acquired by the Piekarski family.[[3]](#cite_note-wolff-3)[[4]](#cite_note-krawczyk30-4)[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5)

By 1528, the village of [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw "Moczydło, Warsaw") was also present to its west, on a road leading to [Imielin](/wiki/Imielin,_Warsaw "Imielin, Warsaw"). The village was owned and inhabited by a [petty nobility](/wiki/Petty_nobility "Petty nobility"). Between 1580 and 1658, it and its adjusted farmlands had an area of approximately 9 hectares, and in 1661, it had 5 houses.[[6]](#cite_note-ew-6)[[7]](#cite_note-malcuzynski-7)[[8]](#cite_note-pawinski-8)

In 1580, Kabaty and their adjusted farmlands had combined area of around 70 ha. In 1656, it was completely destroyed by the Swedish army during the [Second Northern War](/wiki/Northern_War_of_1655%E2%80%931660 "Northern War of 1655–1660").[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5)

In 1721, Kabaty was sold to noblewoman and landowner [Elżbieta Sieniawska](/wiki/El%C5%BCbieta_Sieniawska "Elżbieta Sieniawska"), who incorporated it into the [Wilanów Estate](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w_Estate "Wilanów Estate").[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5) She also acquired Moczydeło, and in 1726, she ordered the protection of the nearby [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods") from [deforestation](/wiki/Deforestation "Deforestation"). As such, wood needed for construction in Kabaty was imported from Sieniawska's estate in [Nieporęt](/wiki/Niepor%C4%99t "Nieporęt").[[9]](#cite_note-nowak-9)[[10]](#cite_note-passa1-10)

In 1775, Kabaty had 16 houses, and in 1827, it had a population of 177 people in 17 houses.[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5) Between 1850 and 1861, the populations of Kabaty and [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw "Moczydło, Warsaw") fought in court to lower the costs of their [feudal duties](/wiki/Feudal_duties "Feudal duties"). Following the [abolition of serfdom](/wiki/Abolition_of_serfdom_in_Poland "Abolition of serfdom in Poland") in 1864, Kabaty was incorporated into the municipality of [Wilanów](/wiki/Gmina_Wilan%C3%B3w "Gmina Wilanów").[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5) In 1892, the village was bought by [Ksawery Branicki](/wiki/Ksawery_Branicki_(1864%E2%80%931926) "Ksawery Branicki (1864–1926)"), who then established there a local woods administration.[[10]](#cite_note-passa1-10)

In 1905, the village was inhabited by 319 people in 38 houses, and in 1920, by 397 people in 59 houses. During the [interwar period](/wiki/Interwar_period "Interwar period"), a [holiday village](/wiki/Holiday_village "Holiday village") was established in the nearby Kabaty Woods, featuring 8 houses and the population of 61 people.[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5) On 31 December 1938, the city of Warsaw bought the [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods"), and dedicated its portion for an urban development.[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) On 11 August 1980, it was given the status of the [nature reserve](/wiki/Nature_reserve "Nature reserve").[[13]](#cite_note-13) Kabaty was incorporated into the city of [Warsaw](/wiki/Warsaw "Warsaw") on 14 May 1951.[[14]](#cite_note-may51-14)

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/2d/Osiedle_SM_Przy_SGGW_Warsaw_2023_KEN.jpg/250px-Osiedle_SM_Przy_SGGW_Warsaw_2023_KEN.jpg)](/wiki/File:Osiedle_SM_Przy_SGGW_Warsaw_2023_KEN.jpg)

The [postmodern](/wiki/Postmodern_architecture "Postmodern architecture") residential buildings at 11 [Przy Bażantarni Street](/wiki/Przy_Ba%C5%BCantarni_Street,_Warsaw "Przy Bażantarni Street, Warsaw"), constructed in 2000.

Between 1987 and 1990, the housing estate of Kabaty, was developed between Jeżewskiego, [Rosoła Street](/wiki/Roso%C5%82a_Street,_Warsaw "Rosoła Street, Warsaw"), [Wąwozowa](/wiki/W%C4%85wozowa_Street,_Warsaw "Wąwozowa Street, Warsaw"), Bronikowskiego, Kiepury, and Dembego Streets. Designed Jacek Jan Nowicki, as part of the urban development of [Natolin](/wiki/Natolin,_Warsaw "Natolin, Warsaw"), the neighbourhood consisted of apartment buildings, built in the [large panel system](/wiki/Large_panel_system_building "Large panel system building") technique. It was further expanded to the west between 1997 and 2002, within an area contained between Jeżewskiego, Kiepury, Bronikowskiego, Wąwozowa, and Wańkowicza Streets.[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-chmielewski-17) Beginning in the 1980s, and continuing throughout the 1990s and 2000s, the area of Kabaty experienced a large development of housing estates with apartment buildings.[[17]](#cite_note-chmielewski-17)[[18]](#cite_note-mazur1-18) In 1995, the [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty_metro_station "Kabaty metro station") station of the [M1](/wiki/M1_(Warsaw) "M1 (Warsaw)") line of [Warsaw Metro](/wiki/Warsaw_Metro "Warsaw Metro") rapid transit underground system, was opened at the intersection of Komisji Edukacji Narodowej Avenue and [Wąwozowa Street](/wiki/W%C4%85wozowa_Street,_Warsaw "Wąwozowa Street, Warsaw").[[19]](#cite_note-dane-19)[[20]](#cite_note-ztm1-20) To the south, next to the Kabaty Woods was also opened the [Kabaty Technical and Storage Station](/wiki/Kabaty_Technical_and_Storage_Station "Kabaty Technical and Storage Station"), a [motive power depot](/wiki/Motive_power_depot "Motive power depot") for the metro system.[[21]](#cite_note-stpk-21)

In 1998, the district of [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") was subdivided into the areas of the [City Information System](/wiki/City_Information_System_(Warsaw) "City Information System (Warsaw)"), with Kabaty becoming one of them. The area additionally included portion of the neighbourhood of Moczydło.[[22]](#cite_note-22)[[23]](#cite_note-23)

Between 1998 and 2000, three [postmodern](/wiki/Postmodern_architecture "Postmodern architecture") apartment buildings were built at 11 and 13 [Przy Bażantarni Street](/wiki/Przy_Ba%C5%BCantarni_Street,_Warsaw "Przy Bażantarni Street, Warsaw"), forming the Przy SGGW Housing Association Neighbourhood.[[24]](#cite_note-asd-24) Between 2006 and 2017, the St. Padre Pio. Church was built at 25 Rybałtów Street, belonging to the [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") denomination.[[25]](#cite_note-church1-25)[[26]](#cite_note-church2-26) In 2017, Rosnowskiego and Korbońskiego Streets were built, connecting Kabaty, and Ursynów at large, with the [Wilanów](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w "Wilanów") district.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

## Characteristics

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kabaty&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Characteristics")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/1/18/Osiedle_Kabaty_od_W%C4%85wozowej_SE.jpg/250px-Osiedle_Kabaty_od_W%C4%85wozowej_SE.jpg)](/wiki/File:Osiedle_Kabaty_od_W%C4%85wozowej_SE.jpg)

The appartments at [Wąwozowa Street](/wiki/W%C4%85wozowa_Street,_Warsaw "Wąwozowa Street, Warsaw") in Kabaty.

Kabaty is a residential area dominated with the high-rise apartment buildings.[[5]](#cite_note-ew301-5)[[28]](#cite_note-sukp-28) In 2024, it had around 30,000 residents.[[29]](#cite_note-29) The area includes the housing estate of Kabaty, located between Jeżewskiego, Wańkowicza, Kiepury, Dembego, [Rosoła](/wiki/Jana_Roso%C5%82a_Street,_Warsaw "Jana Rosoła Street, Warsaw") and [Wąwozowa](/wiki/W%C4%85wozowa_Street,_Warsaw "Wąwozowa Street, Warsaw") Streets.[[30]](#cite_note-30) There is also the Przy SGGW Housing Association Neighbourhood, consisting of three [postmodern](/wiki/Postmodern_architecture "Postmodern architecture") buildings at 11 and 13 [Przy Bażantarni Street](/wiki/Przy_Ba%C5%BCantarni_Street,_Warsaw "Przy Bażantarni Street, Warsaw").[[24]](#cite_note-asd-24) Additionally the edges of the neighbourhood features low-rise single-family housing. This includes the area near the Warsaw Escarpment, to the east of Relaksowa Street, including of the neighbourhood of [Stare Kabaty](/wiki/Stare_Kabaty "Stare Kabaty") (*Old Kabaty*).[[28]](#cite_note-sukp-28)[[31]](#cite_note-31) There is also a small portion of the neighbourhood of [Moczydło](/wiki/Moczyd%C5%82o,_Warsaw "Moczydło, Warsaw"), centred on Ustronie Street in the northwest.[[28]](#cite_note-sukp-28)[[32]](#cite_note-32)

The neighbourhood has the [Kabaty](/wiki/Kabaty_metro_station "Kabaty metro station") station of the [M1](/wiki/M1_(Warsaw) "M1 (Warsaw)") line of [Warsaw Metro](/wiki/Warsaw_Metro "Warsaw Metro") rapid transit underground system, located at the intersection of Komisji Edukacji Narodowej Avenue and Wąwozowa Street, as well as the [Kabaty Technical and Storage Station](/wiki/Kabaty_Technical_and_Storage_Station "Kabaty Technical and Storage Station"), a [motive power depot](/wiki/Motive_power_depot "Motive power depot") for the metro system, located near the [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods").[[19]](#cite_note-dane-19)[[20]](#cite_note-ztm1-20) Additionally, the neighbourhood also includes St. Padre Pio Church at 25 Rybałtów Street, belonging to the [Catholic](/wiki/Catholic_Church "Catholic Church") denomination.[[25]](#cite_note-church1-25)

## Location and boundaries

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kabaty&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Location and boundaries")]

Kabaty is a [City Information System area](/wiki/List_of_districts_and_neighbourhoods_of_Warsaw "List of districts and neighbourhoods of Warsaw") of Warsaw, within the south-eastern portion of the [Ursynów](/wiki/Ursyn%C3%B3w "Ursynów") district. To the north, its border is approximately determined by [Przy Bażantarni Street](/wiki/Przy_Ba%C5%BCantarni_Street,_Warsaw "Przy Bażantarni Street, Warsaw"), Komisji Edukacji Narodowej Avenue, Jeżewskiego Street, [Rosoła Street](/wiki/Jana_Roso%C5%82a_Street,_Warsaw "Jana Rosoła Street, Warsaw"), and around the possessions at 22 Rosoła Street; to the east, by the border of the Ursynów district; to the south, by the [Kabaty Woods](/wiki/Kabaty_Woods "Kabaty Woods"); and to the west, by Ustronie Street, Wełniana Street, [Stryjeńskich Street](/wiki/Stryje%C5%84skich_Street,_Warsaw "Stryjeńskich Street, Warsaw"), and around the possession of the [Kabaty Technical and Storage Station](/wiki/Kabaty_Technical_and_Storage_Station "Kabaty Technical and Storage Station").[[33]](#cite_note-msi-33)

It borders [Natolin](/wiki/Natolin,_Warsaw "Natolin, Warsaw") to the north, [Błonia Wilanowskie](/w/index.php?title=B%C5%82onia_Wilanowskie&action=edit&redlink=1 "Błonia Wilanowskie (page does not exist)") and [Powsin](/wiki/Powsin "Powsin") to the east, and the Kabaty Woods Nature Reserve to the southeast. Its eastern boundary form the border between districts of Ursynów and [Wilanów](/wiki/Wilan%C3%B3w "Wilanów").[[33]](#cite_note-msi-33)

## References

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Kabaty&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: References")]

1. **[^](#cite_ref-handke_1-0)** Kwiryna Handke: *Dzieje Warszawy nazwami pisane*. Warsaw: Warsaw History Museum, 2011, p. 307–308. ISBN 978-83-62189-08-3. (in Polish)
2. **[^](#cite_ref-2)** Marcin Śpiewakowski (3 September 2017). ["Nazwy dzielnic Warszawy. Skąd się wzięły i co oznaczają?"](https://warszawa.naszemiasto.pl/nazwy-dzielnic-warszawy-skad-sie-wziely-i-co-oznaczaja/ar/c11-3645186). *warszawa.naszemiasto.pl* (in Polish).
